rag.description: The SetWindowState action sets the browser window to Maximized, Minimized, or FullScreen by calling the corresponding W3C WebDriver window command (Maximize, Minimize, or Fullscreen). The target state is taken from the Argument property and matched with OrdinalIgnoreCase, so 'maximized', 'MAXIMIZED', and 'Maximized' are all equivalent. If Argument is absent, empty, or does not match any recognized value, all three conditional branches are skipped and the action returns a default plugin response without error — the window state is unchanged. There are no parameters and no output values.

qa.question: What does the SetWindowState action do?
qa.answer: SetWindowState changes the browser window state by calling the W3C WebDriver Maximize, Minimize, or Fullscreen window command depending on the value of the Argument property. The comparison is OrdinalIgnoreCase, so 'maximized', 'MAXIMIZED', and 'Maximized' are all equivalent.

qa.question: What values does the Argument property accept?
qa.answer: Argument accepts three values: 'Maximized' to call WebDriver.Manage().Window.Maximize(), 'Minimized' to call WebDriver.Manage().Window.Minimize(), and 'FullScreen' to call WebDriver.Manage().Window.FullScreen(). All comparisons are OrdinalIgnoreCase.

qa.question: What happens if Argument is absent, empty, or set to an unrecognized value?
qa.answer: If Argument does not match any of the three recognized values — including when it is absent or empty — all three conditional branches are skipped. The action returns a default plugin response without error and the browser window state is unchanged.

qa.question: Does SetWindowState produce any output parameters?
qa.answer: No. SetWindowState returns a default plugin response with no output parameters. The window state is changed via the driver but not echoed back into the session context.

qa.question: What W3C WebDriver endpoints does SetWindowState use?
qa.answer: It uses POST /session/{session id}/window/maximize for Maximized, POST /session/{session id}/window/minimize for Minimized, and POST /session/{session id}/window/fullscreen for FullScreen.

qa.question: What is the manifest version for SetWindowState?
What is the namespace and plugin type for SetWindowState?
qa.answer: The namespace is G4.Plugins.Ui and the pluginType is Action.

qa.question: Why does SetWindowState not throw when Argument is absent or unrecognized?
qa.answer: All three state branches are conditional checks against the Argument value. When none of them match — because Argument is absent, empty, or an unrecognized string — all branches are skipped and the action returns a default plugin response normally. This is a deliberate safe no-op design.

description: ### Purpose
Sets the browser window to `Maximized`, `Minimized`, or `FullScreen` by calling the corresponding W3C WebDriver window command.
The `Argument` property specifies the target state and is evaluated with `OrdinalIgnoreCase`, so `maximized`, `MAXIMIZED`, and `Maximized` are all valid.
If `Argument` is absent, empty, or does not match any recognized value, the action returns a default response without error — the window state is not changed.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                | Description                                                                                                                  |
| Maximize               | Calls `WebDriver.Manage().Window.Maximize()` to expand the browser window to fill the screen.                                |
| Minimize               | Calls `WebDriver.Manage().Window.Minimize()` to collapse the browser window to the taskbar.                                  |
| Full Screen            | Calls `WebDriver.Manage().Window.FullScreen()` to enter true full-screen mode, hiding OS and browser chrome.                 |
| Case-Insensitive Match | State names are compared with `OrdinalIgnoreCase` — `fullscreen`, `FullScreen`, and `FULLSCREEN` all resolve to full screen. |
| Safe No-Op on Mismatch | An unrecognized or absent `Argument` value skips all branches and returns normally without throwing an exception.            |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                   | Description                                                                                                            |
| Workflow Normalization     | Maximize the browser window before starting a workflow to ensure consistent element visibility and layout.             |
| Background Task Management | Minimize the browser window to move it out of view while other applications or steps are active.                       |
| Kiosk Mode                 | Switch to `FullScreen` to present a page without OS or browser chrome, simulating a kiosk or presentation environment. |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case                    | Description                                                                                                            |
| Consistent Screenshot State | Maximize the window before taking screenshots to avoid layout shifts caused by non-standard window sizes.              |
| Minimize Interaction Test   | Minimize the window and verify that subsequent element interactions behave as expected when the window is not visible. |
| Full-Screen Layout Test     | Enter full-screen mode to verify that the application layout adapts correctly when OS and browser toolbars are hidden. |

edge_cases: Window is already maximized — Maximize() is called again; the driver accepts the call without error and the state remains maximized
Window is currently minimized — Maximize() restores and expands the window; the window becomes the active foreground window
Window is currently in FullScreen mode — Maximize() exits full-screen before applying maximized state on most browsers
Argument is matched case-insensitively — 'maximized', 'MAXIMIZED', and 'Maximized' all route to the same Maximize() call
annotations.expected_result: The browser window is expanded to fill the screen in maximized state.
annotations.notes: The Argument value 'Maximized' triggers WebDriver.Manage().Window.Maximize(). The comparison is OrdinalIgnoreCase so any casing is accepted.

description: ### Maximize the browser window
Sets the browser window to maximized state by calling `WebDriver.Manage().Window.Maximize()`.
The `Argument` value `Maximized` is matched case-insensitively.

edge_cases: Window is already minimized — Minimize() is called again; the driver accepts the call without error and the state remains minimized
Window is in FullScreen mode — Minimize() exits full-screen before minimizing on most browsers
Minimized window does not respond to element interactions — actions targeting DOM elements after minimizing may fail if the driver requires the window to be visible
Argument is matched case-insensitively — 'minimized', 'MINIMIZED', and 'Minimized' all route to the same Minimize() call
annotations.expected_result: The browser window is collapsed to the taskbar in minimized state.
annotations.notes: The Argument value 'Minimized' triggers WebDriver.Manage().Window.Minimize(). The comparison is OrdinalIgnoreCase so any casing is accepted.

description: ### Minimize the browser window
Collapses the browser window to the taskbar by calling `WebDriver.Manage().Window.Minimize()`.
The `Argument` value `Minimized` is matched case-insensitively.

edge_cases: Window is already in full-screen mode — FullScreen() is called again; the driver accepts the call without error and the state remains full screen
Browser security restrictions may prevent full-screen from taking effect in some test environments or headless drivers
FullScreen hides OS chrome and browser toolbars — layout-dependent tests may see different element positions compared to maximized mode
Argument is matched case-insensitively — 'fullscreen', 'FULLSCREEN', and 'FullScreen' all route to the same FullScreen() call
annotations.expected_result: The browser window enters full-screen mode, hiding OS and browser chrome.
annotations.notes: The Argument value 'FullScreen' triggers WebDriver.Manage().Window.FullScreen(). The comparison is OrdinalIgnoreCase so any casing is accepted.

description: ### Enter full-screen mode
Switches the browser window to full-screen mode by calling `WebDriver.Manage().Window.FullScreen()`.
The `Argument` value `FullScreen` is matched case-insensitively.
Argument specifies the target browser window state.
Accepted values are `Maximized`, `Minimized`, and `FullScreen`.
The comparison is case-insensitive — `fullscreen`, `FullScreen`, and `FULLSCREEN` are all valid.
If Argument is absent, empty, or set to an unrecognized value, no state change occurs and the action returns normally without error.
properties.name: Argument

description: Expands the browser window to fill the screen by calling `WebDriver.Manage().Window.Maximize()`.
values.name: Maximized

description: Collapses the browser window to the taskbar by calling `WebDriver.Manage().Window.Minimize()`.
values.name: Minimized

description: Switches the browser window to full-screen mode, hiding OS and browser chrome, by calling `WebDriver.Manage().Window.FullScreen()`.
values.name: FullScreen

protocol.w3c: POST /session/{session id}/window/maximize | POST /session/{session id}/window/minimize | POST /session/{session id}/window/fullscreen

summary: SetWindowState sets the browser window to `Maximized`, `Minimized`, or `FullScreen` by calling the corresponding W3C WebDriver window command.
The `Argument` property selects the target state and is matched case-insensitively; an unrecognized or absent value is a safe no-op that returns normally without error.

In RPA it normalizes window visibility and supports kiosk-mode presentation; in automation testing it enforces a consistent window state before screenshots, layout checks, or full-screen layout verification.
